---

## Version 2026.7

This is a minor bugfix/maintenance release with a few bug fixes and minor security upgrades.

### All Changes

**Changed**

- **Removed `BasicAuthentication` from the default Django REST Framework authentication classes.**
  Nothing built-in ever used basic auth, and this is a slightly safer default configuration for most projects.
- **Improved how CSRF token access works if you change `CSRF_COOKIE_NAME`**.
  Previously, `csrftoken` was hardcoded in multiple places in the front end, so if you changed that setting it would break.
  Added a `getCsrfToken()` helper that reads from the server, so changing `CSRF_COOKIE_NAME` works everywhere.
  - This relies on a new `csrf_settings` context processor to populate `settings.CSRF_COOKIE_NAME` to templates.
- Upgraded dependencies.

**Fixed**

- **Slug uniqueness is now checked through the model's `_default_manager` rather than `objects`.**
  Models that follow the [strict team access](/teams/#strict-team-access) pattern—where `objects` is replaced with a
  team-scoped manager—now still enforce globally-unique slugs across teams.
- **Slug generation now truncates values to the slug field's `max_length`.** Previously a very long name
  could overflow an associated slug column and error on save.
  - Also fixed an edge case where an auto-generated slug could contain a doubled hyphen (e.g. `foo--2`).
- **Fixed team-context reset handling in `TeamsMiddleware`.** There was a bug in the logic that attempted to reset team context after setting it
  in the middleware that resulted in it always being cleared. In practice, this never mattered but it could manifest in tests that exercised the middleware.
  Also fixed a revealed issue with asynchronous (ASGI) requests attempting to reset the context from an inaccessible token.
- **Fixed the home-page redirect for users with a single pending team invitation.** The condition was inverted, so users
  were only auto-redirected to accept an invitation when they had more than one (and were sent to an arbitrary one). It now
  redirects when there is exactly one open invitation.
- **Hardened the admin dashboard date filters.** Malformed or missing `start`/`end` query parameters now fall back to the
  default date range instead of raising an error.
- **Revoking an API key with an unknown prefix now shows an error message** instead of raising an unhandled exception.
- The AI email tool now calls Django's `send_mail` via `sync_to_async`, so sending email from the async tool no longer errors.
- The chat-naming background task no longer errors if the chat was deleted before the task runs.
- Slug generation now returns an empty base value as-is (e.g. a name with no ASCII characters that slugifies to nothing)
  rather than producing a bare `-2`, leaving the fallback to the caller.
- Fixed a timezone comparison in the profile view (the user's timezone is now compared against the active timezone as a string).

**Upgrading**

- If your project relied on DRF basic auth you should reject the change to remove it from settings.
